subject: Mathematics
title: What Is a Mathematical Proof?
description: One-minute skill: What Is a Mathematical Proof?.

hook.text: In math, 'I checked a million cases' is not proof. You need a logical chain from axioms to conclusion.

buildup.text: A proof is a sequence of statements, each following logically from previous ones or from axioms.

discovery.text: Unlike science, math proofs are absolute. Once proven, a theorem is true forever—no new evidence overturns it.

twist.text: But proofs can have errors. Some 'proofs' stood for decades before a mistake was found.

climax.text: The standard of proof in mathematics is the highest in any human discipline.

punchline.text: In math, a proof means forever. Nothing else comes close.

quiz.question: Why are mathematical proofs considered absolute?
options: They follow logically from axioms and cannot be overturned by evidence
